content:
- task_switch.sh finished and removed current_task.txt; current_tasks/current_tasks_done/task_history were updated as expected.
- Staging all tracked and untracked work now, including .ralph metadata and the progress log, before creating the final task-finished commit.

- Created the final task-finished commit; the post-commit hook is now running the mdBook/docs publish flow.
- Waiting for the hook to complete before pushing.

- Commit 56654de completed successfully and the post-commit docs publish pushed the mdBook sync commit to pgtuskmaster-docs.
- Pushing the main repository commit now to finish the task end-to-end.

- Final status check found follow-on .ralph updates after the first commit: current_tasks/current_tasks_done plus progress/195.jsonl.
- Inspecting the exact metadata diff now, then I will package and push a cleanup commit so the branch is actually clean.

- Read the progress log and scanned .ralph/current_tasks.md plus .ralph/current_tasks_done.md.
- Resolved blockers from current_tasks_done: no active bug tasks exist in the active list; the unblocked failing candidates include story-ctl task 08 (high), story-general-architecture tasks 01/02 (medium), and low-priority tasks 09 and logging.
- Next I am verifying the selected task file exists, then I will write the chosen path into .ralph/current_task.txt and stop immediately.

- Verified the selected task file exists and wrote the choice to .ralph/current_task.txt.
- Chosen task: .ralph/tasks/story-ctl-operator-experience/08-task-replace-hand-rolled-api-server-with-axum-axum-server-and-tower.md.
- Reason: no active bug tasks were present; among unblocked failing tasks, task 08 is the highest priority (`high`) and therefore outranks the unblocked medium and low priority tasks.

- Read previous progress and loaded .ralph/current_task.txt.
- Active task is .ralph/tasks/story-ctl-operator-experience/08-task-replace-hand-rolled-api-server-with-axum-axum-server-and-tower.md.
- Reading the task file now and checking git status so I can determine whether this turn is design-only (TO BE VERIFIED) or execution (NOW EXECUTE).

- Read the active task file. It has no NOW EXECUTE marker, so this turn is the design/type pass.
- Next I am inspecting existing task conventions plus the api/config/tls code to redesign the types first before fixing any compile errors.

- The task is in design mode, so I wrote a short plan and started reading runtime/tls/config files plus an example Ralph task that already reached NOW EXECUTE.
- I also delegated one focused explorer to summarize the transport-shaped types that should be replaced first.

- Current design target is becoming clearer: break apart ApiWorkerCtx, simplify API TLS mode, and introduce typed server/auth/reload state instead of transport fields.
- Reading the rest of parser/runtime/api worker now before I commit to the all-at-once type rewrite.

- I now have the validator and the rest of the manual HTTP server loaded.
- Checking all use sites of the API TLS/config/server types so the next edit can rewrite the type layer across the affected modules in one shot.

- Inspected the active API rewrite task plus src/runtime/node.rs, src/tls.rs, src/config/schema.rs, src/config/parser.rs, and a minimal slice of src/api/worker.rs for naming accuracy.
- Main hotspots so far: ApiConfig.listen_addr as raw SocketAddr, ApiTlsMode optional/required/disabled, TlsServerConfig and TlsClientAuthConfig remaining schema-shaped instead of server-plan ADTs, and runtime/node directly binding TcpListener and mutating ApiWorkerCtx.
- Next I am finishing parser validation review and then I will write a concise redesign report focused on what should become axum/axum-server application/server/tls state instead of transport plumbing.

- Repo-wide grep shows ApiTlsMode/TlsServerConfig are shared between API, postgres materialization, CLI config display, parser, runtime, and test harness fixtures.
- Reading those exact helpers and fixtures now so the one-shot type redesign does not leave mismatched config shapes.

- Finished the design-only inspection and extracted the main transport-shaped seams across runtime/node, tls, schema, and parser.
- Strongest conclusion: API TLS/auth/listener config currently survives too far into runtime as shared schema-shaped types, and api/postgres still share one TLS shape even though the API now needs axum-server-specific reload and CN-allow-list semantics.
- Final answer will recommend: split API TLS from Postgres TLS, replace ApiTlsMode optional/required/disabled with an API transport ADT, keep InlineOrPath/SecretSource at parse boundary only, and let runtime/node build ApiAppState plus a single server future instead of TcpListener + ApiWorkerCtx mutation.

- Completed the type-first design sweep across schema/config/api/runtime/tls.
- Main changes: removed the old Optional API TLS concept from the type model, introduced explicit API transport/TLS/client-cert ADTs, added API server state types, and shifted runtime construction toward a server-plan shape.
- Next is to write the handoff plan into the task file, mark it NOW EXECUTE, and stop without fixing compile errors.

- Wrote the execution handoff into the active task file and marked it NOW EXECUTE.
- This turn intentionally stopped at the type-design layer: schema/runtime/api/tls types were reshaped first and the repository is expected not to compile yet.
- The next turn should start from the NOW EXECUTE plan, finish the parser/runtime/server migration onto axum/axum-server/tower, repair compile errors, run the required make targets, then do docs with k2-docs-loop only after all tests pass.

- Read prior Ralph progress and current_task pointer.
- Confirmed the active task is the axum/axum-server API rewrite task and prior turn already switched it to NOW EXECUTE.
- Next I am reading the task body and repo status, then I will implement/fix compile errors against that plan and validate with the required make targets.

- Inspected src/api/worker.rs, src/api/controller.rs, src/runtime/node.rs, src/tls.rs, src/config/schema.rs, src/config/parser.rs, docs/src/reference/http-api.md, and tests/bdd_api_http.rs for the axum worker integration question.
- Concrete conclusions: runtime already builds ApiServerCtx with bind/identity/runtime_config/dcs_handle/state/auth/transport/cert_reloader/log, but auth is currently wired as Disabled and cert_reloader is a zero-sized placeholder.
- Reuse surface is limited: controller.rs has the /state and /switchover business logic; tls.rs builds rustls server transport and client-cert policy; worker.rs only has a boolean peer-cert presence helper, not common-name inspection or cert reload.

- Inspected the repo for step_once/raw-socket dependencies before the axum rewrite lands.
- Confirmed the direct break in requested scope is tests/bdd_api_http.rs: it imports ApiWorkerCtx, binds a TcpListener, calls ctx.local_addr(), and manually drives pgtuskmaster_rust::api::worker::step_once.
- Confirmed tests/cli_binary.rs does not depend on api::worker at all; its local spawn_single_request_server helper is only a CLI outbound request stub and can stay unchanged.
- Read runtime/node, api/controller, api/mod, and test_harness/runtime_config to identify the smallest replacement seam.
- Minimal recommendation: rewrite tests/bdd_api_http.rs to use an axum-era integration helper that builds the real router/app state and drives requests through tower::ServiceExt::oneshot (preferred) or a spawned server future; add one narrow src/test_harness/api.rs helper plus src/test_harness/mod.rs export if integration tests still need a public seam.
- RuntimeConfigBuilder in src/test_harness/runtime_config.rs already provides the config knobs these tests need; no change required there unless a new helper wants a tiny convenience constructor.

- Collected the execution-phase compile errors with make check.
- Confirmed the current worktree is mid-migration: new API transport/client-auth ADTs exist, but parser/CLI/tests/postgres-managed still reference the deleted ApiTlsMode and api/worker is still the old hand-rolled server.
- Next I am replacing api/worker with the axum server, then I will sweep the remaining callers onto ApiTransportConfig/TlsServerConfig enums and re-run make check.

- Subtask scope only: updated src/cli/config.rs, src/postgres_managed.rs, and src/test_harness/runtime_config.rs off the deleted ApiTlsMode/api.security.tls shape.
- CLI now derives HTTPS from api.security.transport and only requires pgtm API client cert/key when ApiClientAuthConfig::Required is configured; postgres_managed now matches enum TlsServerConfig::{Disabled,Enabled}; runtime_config samples/tests now use ApiTransportConfig and enum TlsServerConfig.
- I am running make check once to confirm these three files are clean and to surface any remaining compile failures outside this subtask.

- As a subagent, updated src/config/parser.rs for the ApiTransportConfig/TlsServerConfig enum migration.
- Reworked runtime validation to use api.security.transport, added API transport/client-auth validation, switched postgres TLS checks to enum matching, and updated parser tests/base configs to the new shapes.
- I did not run full validation from the subagent; the parent agent should rerun make check because other files still need coordinated fixes.

- Replaced the manual API worker with an axum/axum-server router, auth middleware, and cert reload path.
- Integrated the worker-returned config sweeps: parser, CLI config, postgres managed TLS materialization, and runtime-config test fixtures now compile against ApiTransportConfig/TlsServerConfig enums.
- make check now passes; next I am running lint and then the nextest suites, after which docs need the k2-docs-loop.

- Resumed the axum API rewrite task in NOW EXECUTE state and refreshed progress/task/worktree context.
- Confirmed make check/lint/test had already passed earlier and isolated make test-long failures to stale checked-in HA/docker/runtime config fixtures that still use the removed TLS schema (`mode = \"required\"`, `require_client_cert`, and `api.security.tls`).
- Next I am patching those fixtures to the new postgres TLS enum and API transport/auth shape, then rerunning make test-long before touching docs.

- Patched the checked-in HA givens and docker runtime configs off the removed TLS shapes.
- Postgres fixture configs now use `mode = "enabled"` plus `client_certificate = "optional"`; API fixture configs now use `security.transport = { transport = "https", ... }` with legacy input-side `require_client_cert` only where API mTLS is required.
- Next I am rerunning `make test-long` to confirm the stale-config failure is gone before doing the docs pass.

- The updated fixtures got make test-long past the old runtime-config parse failure; the suite now fails later at scenario execution.
- I am inspecting the exported nextest JUnit/log artifacts under target/nextest/ultra-long to isolate the concrete failing behavior before making more changes.
- If this is another stale fixture or API behavior mismatch, I will patch that next and rerun the long suite.

- Investigated the remaining single long-test failure after the axum rewrite.
- Key finding so far: the HA worker does not consume peer `/state` responses at all; failover decisions are built from local pginfo + DCS member records in `src/ha/worker.rs`, and DCS member records are written from local pginfo in `src/dcs/worker.rs`.
- `/state` itself appears live rather than stale because the axum handler reads `StateSubscriber::latest()` on every request. I am still checking auth/TLS/server-lifetime angles before concluding the regression is outside the API rewrite surface.

- Traced the remaining HA failure to leader-lease lifecycle rather than API handler behavior.
- Added a targeted DCS worker fix: when a node drops its own stale member record, it now also releases its owned leader lease, plus a regression test for that stale-snapshot path.
- The focused build/test compiles; next I am rerunning the single failing ultra-long HA scenario before rerunning the full make targets.

- The focused ultra-long rerun for `ha_primary_storage_stalled_then_new_primary_takes_over` completed successfully after the DCS leader-lease fix.
- That confirms the remaining failure was in the stale-member/owned-leader-lease path, not the axum handler path.
- Next I am running the full required validation sequence: make check, make lint, make test, and make test-long, then docs via k2-docs-loop.

- Full validation is now green: make check, make lint, make test, and make test-long all passed after the leader-lease fix.
- Moving into the required docs phase now, using the k2-docs-loop skill after test completion as directed by AGENTS.md.
- Next I am updating the runtime-configuration/TLS/API docs and examples to match the new transport/TLS schema, then I will finish the Ralph task metadata, commit, and push.

- Re-read the active task and worktree before finalization.
- Verified the implementation and validation are already green, but found one remaining acceptance/docs gap: docs/src/reference/http-api.md still omitted POST /reload/certs.
- Next I am patching the HTTP API reference and then updating the task file metadata/checklists before running task_switch, commit, and push.

- Patched the last acceptance gaps instead of only flipping task metadata.
- Added explicit coverage for successful live /state, invalid auth, read-vs-admin auth, HTTPS reload/certs success, wrong client CA rejection, and wrong common-name rejection.
- Updated docs/src/reference/http-api.md to include POST /reload/certs, and marked the task checklist/pass state complete pending final make reruns, task_switch, commit, and push.

- Final validation rerun is green on the finished tree: make check, make lint, make test, make test-long, plus docs-lint/docs-build/docs-hygiene all passed.
- Added the last missing acceptance coverage/doc line and revalidated instead of relying on earlier green results.
- Next I am running task_switch, staging everything including .ralph metadata, committing with the required task-finished message, and pushing.
